audit_items.proof_trace: do_item_get() at line 3265 increments the item refcount (items.c:666); every other error return in the function calls do_item_remove(it) before returning, but the nbytes<=2 guard at line 3271-3273 returns NON_NUMERIC without releasing the reference. The caller add_delta() (thread.c:580) does not call do_item_remove after do_add_delta returns, so the leaked reference is never reclaimed, preventing item_free() from ever being called on that slab slot.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A client stores a key with an empty or minimal value (e.g., SET key 0 0 2\r\n\r\n so nbytes==2), then sends repeated INCR/DECR commands on that key; each request leaks one reference count, the item's refcount grows unboundedly, and the slab slot is permanently unavailable, enabling memory exhaustion via external requests.
